author: śrīla raghunāthadāsa gosvāmī
verses:
vṛṣabha⋅danuja⋅nāśān⋅antaraṁ jat sva⋅goṣṭhīm
ayasi vṛṣabha⋅śatro mā spṛśa tvaṁ vadantyām
iti vṛṣa⋅ravi⋅putryāṁ kṛṣṇa⋅pārṣṇiṁ prakhātaṁ
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

tri⋅jagati nivasad jat tīrtha⋅vṛndaṁ tamoghnaṁ
vraja⋅nṛpati⋅kumāreṇāhṛtaṁ tat samagram
svayam idam avagāḍhaṁ jan mahimnaḥ prakāśaṁ
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

jad ati⋅vimala⋅nīre tīrtha⋅rūpe praśaste
tvam api kuru kṛśāṅgi! snānam atraiva rādhe
iti vinaya⋅vacobhiḥ prārthanā⋅kṛt sa kṛṣṇas
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

vṛṣabha⋅danuja⋅nāśād uttha⋅pāpaṁ samāptaṁ
dyumaṇi⋅sakha⋅jayoccair varjayitveti tīrthaṁ
nijam akhila⋅sakhībhiḥ kuṇḍam eva prakāśyaṁ
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

jad ati sakala⋅tīrthais tyakta⋅vākyaiḥ prabhītaiḥ
sa⋅vinayam abhiyuktaiḥ kṛṣṇa⋅candre nivedya
agatika⋅gati⋅rādhā varjanān no gatiḥ kā
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

jad ati⋅vikala⋅tīrthaṁ kṛṣṇa⋅candraṁ prasusthaṁ
ati⋅laghu⋅nati⋅vākyaiḥ suprasanneti rādhā
vividha⋅caṭula⋅vākyaiḥ prārthanāḍhyā bhavantī
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

jad ati⋅lalita⋅pādais tāṁ prasādyāpta⋅tair thyais
tad atiśaya⋅kṛpārdraiḥ saṅgamena praviṣṭaiḥ
vraja⋅nava⋅yuva⋅rādhā⋅kuṇḍam eva prapannaṁ
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

jad ati⋅nikaṭa⋅tīre klapta⋅kuñjaṁ suramyaṁ
subala⋅baṭu⋅mukhebhyo rādhikādyaiḥ pradattam
vividha⋅kusuma⋅vallī⋅kalpa⋅vṛkṣādi⋅rājaṁ
tad ati⋅vimala⋅nīraṁ śyāma⋅kuṇḍaṁ gatir me

paripaṭhati sumedhāḥ śyāma⋅kuṇḍāṣṭakaṁ jo
nava⋅jaladhara⋅rūpe svarṇa⋅kāntyāṁ ca rāgāt
vraja⋅narapati⋅putras tasya labhyaḥ suśīghraṁ
saha sa⋅gaṇa⋅sakhībhī rādhayā syāt subhajyaḥ
en_translation:
After the slaying of the bull demon, when Rādhā told Lord Kṛṣṇa, "O enemy of the bull! Do not touch Me!", Kṛṣṇa dug this pond with His heel. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

All the holy places in the three worlds that destroy darkness were brought together by the Prince of Vraja. He personally immersed Himself in this pond, whose glories are now manifest. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

Lord Kṛṣṇa, with humble words, requested Rādhā: "O slender-limbed one! O Rādhā! Please take a bath here in this pure water, which is praised as a holy place." That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

Any sin arising from the slaying of the bull demon was considered finished by Lord Kṛṣṇa after He bathed here. But Rādhā, having victoriously rejected this holy place, manifested Her own pond along with all Her friends. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

When all other holy places, terrified and speechless, humbly approached the moon-like Kṛṣṇa and submitted: "If Rādhā, the refuge of the helpless, avoids us, then what refuge do we have?", the Lord showed His mercy. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

When the other holy places were extremely distressed and Lord Kṛṣṇa was present, Rādhā became greatly pleased by their very humble words and prostrations, and then She Herself became rich with various playful prayers to the Lord. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

Having pleased Her with their humble approach, all the holy places obtained Her own pond. They entered with excessive mercy, gathered together, and took refuge in the pond of the youthful Rādhā of Vraja. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

On its nearby bank, charming groves are arranged. These groves were given to Subala and the Lord's other friends by Śrīmati Rādhikā and Her companions. It is a royal place, abounding with various flowers, creepers, and desire trees. That very pure water of Śyāma-kuṇḍa is my refuge.

Whoever, being intelligent, recites these eight verses in praise of Śyāma-kuṇḍa with love for Him who is like a fresh cloud and for Her with golden effulgence, quickly attains the Son of the King of Vraja. He will become worshipable by Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa and Their gopī companions.
